title: Stochastic Path Sampler For Lattice Field Theory
authors: Shiyang Chen; Moxian Qian; Gert Aarts; Biagio Lucini; Kai Zhou
problem: Data-free sampling of an unnormalized lattice target while controlling long autocorrelation.
method: Learn independent forward and backward Langevin drifts with a path KL; correct proposals by extended-space trajectory IMH.
experiment: 2D Lx8 phi4; L=16,32,48,64; HMC, SPS, SPS+IMH.
headline_result: Corrected observables agree with the tested HMC benchmark; autocorrelation is shorter in unmatched update units.
author_limitation: Support coverage, scaling, and matched total cost remain open.
reviewer_inference: The supported delta is the lattice implementation plus extended-space trajectory IMH, not path KL or paired drifts alone.
safe_sentence: SPS adapts paired stochastic paths to lattice field theory and corrects finite-training proposals with extended-space trajectory IMH.
prohibited_sentence: SPS invented path-space training or paired forward/backward drifts.
next_search: matched cost; support stress test; gauge fields

title: Path Integral Sampler: a stochastic control approach for sampling
problem: Finite-time sampling of an unnormalized target without a long-mixing chain.
method: Train one controlled Brownian path by a Schrodinger-bridge cost; calibrate with trajectory importance weights.
key_equation: D_KL(Q^u||Q*) = expected control energy plus terminal cost
experiment: Bayesian inference and normalization benchmarks against MCMC, SMC, and variational methods.
headline_result: PIS is competitive on the reported suite and provides weighted calibration.
author_limitation: Training overhead, finite-control error, discretization, and weight variance.
reviewer_inference: Correction is trajectory weighting, not trajectory IMH.
safe_sentence: PIS already supplies data-free path control and full-trajectory importance correction.
prohibited_sentence: SPS introduced data-free stochastic path training.

title: Denoising Diffusion Samplers
authors: Francisco Vargas; Will Grathwohl; Arnaud Doucet
problem: Data-free diffusion sampling without target-distributed training data.
method: Learn the reverse of a target-noising OU process by reverse path KL and an invariant-preserving discretization.
key_equation: KL(Q_theta||P) = control energy plus endpoint target ratio
experiment: Funnel, 1600-dimensional LGCP, and multimodal flow target against PIS, SMC, and VI.
headline_result: DDS is competitive and more stable than PIS in the reported settings.
author_limitation: Training cost and score-error assumptions limit broad conclusions.
reviewer_inference: Endpoint draws are approximate; the paper gives an importance normalizer, not trajectory IMH.
safe_sentence: DDS already supplies reverse path-KL training and tractable trajectory ratios.
next_search: CMCD; OU reference on lattice targets

title: Transport meets Variational Inference: Controlled Monte Carlo Diffusions
authors: Francisco Vargas; Shreyas Padhy; Denis Blessing; Nikolas Nuesken
problem: Reduce instability and weight variance in annealed diffusion sampling.
method: Jointly adapt score-coupled forward and backward drifts with a path divergence and trajectory weight.
experiment: Annealed inference suite and normalizer tests against ULA, MCD, UHA, LDVI, PIS, DDS, and SMC.
headline_result: CMCD improves reported ELBO and normalizer estimates across the tested suite.
author_limitation: Requires a prescribed tractable annealing path; finite-trained endpoints remain approximate.
reviewer_inference: Paired drifts predate SPS; CMCD couples them by the intermediate score and uses weights rather than IMH.
safe_sentence: CMCD already learns both forward and backward diffusion dynamics through a path-space objective.
prohibited_sentence: SPS was first to learn paired stochastic drifts.
next_search: trajectory IMH; independent versus score-coupled drifts

title: Stochastic normalizing flows as non-equilibrium transformations
authors: Michele Caselle; Elia Cellini; Alessandro Nada; Marco Panero
problem: Combine trainable maps with stochastic nonequilibrium updates for lattice sampling and free energies.
method: Alternate affine flow blocks and detailed-balance stochastic blocks; train and estimate through a forward/reverse path ratio.
key_equation: Dtilde_KL(q0 Pf||p Pr) = -E log weight + log(Z/Z0)
experiment: 2D phi4, 16x8 through 64x8, pure stochastic paths, flows, and SNFs.
headline_result: Hybrid SNFs improve reported ESS and free-energy error in the tested setups.
author_limitation: Cost accounting omits training and some deterministic costs; conclusions are architecture- and protocol-specific.
reviewer_inference: The main estimator is trajectory reweighting; ordinary independent MH is only mentioned as an alternative for deterministic flows.
safe_sentence: SNF already combines lattice stochastic paths, learned maps, path ratios, and exact weighted observables.
prohibited_sentence: SPS introduced stochastic lattice paths or trajectory ratios.
